content.full_text:
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Sirs;

I am elected to the Board of Directors of the South Bay Hospital District.
After reviewing your memorandum dated August 22, 1990, I am not certain
that our "Amended Conflict of Interest Code of the South Bay Hospital
District of Los Angeles County" approved by our Board of Directors on
October 4, 1990 complies with the mandates of the memorandum.
The Board leases the South Bay Hospital to American Medical International
for 3 million dollars per year. The Board's primary purpose at this time
is to invest the lease income and property tax revenues and to allocate
more than 1 million dollars annually in direct grants to non-profit agencies
providing health related services to residents of the district.
The Board has appointed a Finance Advisory Committee to advise us on
investment procedures etc. I find it confusing that our legal counsel
have given an opinion that this Committee does not have to comply with the
Conflict of Interest Code and file form 730 disclosure statement in spite
of the fact that Government Code Section 54952.3 specifically includes
advisory committees as part of the legislative body. The Board must
approve all investment and disbursement of public funds. Due to this fact,
I feel that the Board Executive Director of Finance and
purchases and legal counsel are required to file form 721 disclosure
statements.
I specifically state legal counsel because our current attorney of record
represents the South Bay Hospital District (grantor) and Hospital Home
Health Care Agency (grantee) parent of a non-profit corporation that has
received more than 1 million dollars in grants from the Board. Government
Code Section 54957, second paragraph, defines legal counsel as an employee.
Enclosed please find the very confusing and expensive, legal gobbleygook
prepared by the Board's legal counsel and the very simple and straight
forward resolution no.7273 adopted by the Redondo Beach City Council on
September 18, 1990.

Sincerely,

Eva Snow, Director

encl: Board's conflict of interest code
City of Redondo Beach Resolution no. 7273
Easy Reader article dated: 11/8/1990

700 North Pacific Coast Highway
Suite 206
Redondo Beach, California 90277
(213) 374-3426 FAX (213) 376-4738

BOARD OF DIRECTORS
Virginia D. Fischer
Ken Johnson
Jean G. McMillan
James M. Rieker
Eva T. Snow

DIRECTOR EMERITUS
Gerald R. Witt

EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR
Philip Valera

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from Eva Snow, Director of the South Bay Hospital District, to the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission. She expresses confusion about whether the "Amended Conflict of Interest Code of the South Bay Hospital District of Los Angeles County" approved by their Board of Directors on October 4, 1990, complies with the mandates of the memorandum she received. She explains that the Board leases the South Bay Hospital to American Medical International for $3 million per year and allocates more than $1 million annually in direct grants to non-profit agencies providing health-related services to residents of the district. The Board has appointed a Finance Advisory Committee to advise them on investment procedures, etc. Snow notes that her legal counsel has given an opinion that this committee does not have to comply with the Conflict of Interest Code and file form 730 disclosure statement, despite Government Code Section 54952.3 specifically including advisory committees as part of the legislative body. She states that the Board must approve all investment and disbursement of public funds and that she feels the Board, its Executive Director, Director of Finance and Purchasing, and legal counsel are required to file form 721 disclosure statements. She specifically states legal counsel because her current attorney of record represents the South Bay Hospital District (grantor) and Hospital Home Health Care Agency (grantee) parent of a non-profit corporation that has received more than $1 million dollars in grants from the Board. Government Code Section 54957, second paragraph, defines legal counsel as an employee. Enclosed are two documents: one prepared by the Board's legal counsel and another, a very simple and straightforward resolution no. 7273 adopted by the Redondo Beach City Council on September 18, 1990. Sincerely, Eva Snow, Director encl: Board's conflict of interest code City of Redondo Beach Resolution no. 7273 Easy Reader article dated: 11/8/1990

I specifically state legal counsel because our current attorney of record represents the South Bay Hospital District (grantor) and Hospital Home Health Care Agency (grantee) parent of a non-profit corporation that has received more than 1 million dollars in grants from the Board. Government Code Section 54957, second paragraph, defines legal counsel as an employee. Enclosed please find the very confusing and expensive, legal gobbleygook prepared by the Board's legal counsel and the very simple and straight forward resolution no.7273 adopted by the Redondo Beach City Council on September 18, 1990.
